answer:
Likely no statutory damages for this infringement. 17 USC §412: statutory damages & attorney's fees available only if registration predates infringement start, or within 3 mo of 1st publication. Mid-Jan pub → grace window closed ~mid-Apr 2026. Infringement began before registration → actual damages + infringer's profits only (often hard to prove for piracy).

Still do:
1. Register now — required before filing suit at all (Fourth Estate v. Wall-Street.com), covers statutory damages vs future infringements by new infringers.
2. DMCA takedown to pirate site + host + Google — no registration needed, usually fastest fix.
3. If infringement continues post-registration by same infringer, courts generally treat it as continuation of pre-registration infringement → still no statutory damages, so don't count on that workaround.

Caveat: consult IP counsel to confirm exact infringement start date vs any registration facts.
